title: RSVP collaborative BCI dataset from Zheng et al 2020
description: A collaborative brain-computer interface dataset based on rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP) paradigm, comprising synchronized EEG recordings from 14 healthy subjects (7 pairs) performing target image detection tasks. The dataset includes 62-channel EEG data sampled at 1000 Hz across 2 sessions with 3 runs each, annotated with p300 event-related potentials for target and non-target visual stimuli. This cross-session dataset supports the development and evaluation of collaborative BCI systems and machine learning classifiers for target detection in visual search tasks.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Neuroscan Synamps2 system at 1000 Hz sampling rate from 62 electrodes in standard 10-20 montage with vertex (Cz) reference. Subjects performed RSVP-based target detection tasks with 100 ms stimulus onset asynchrony, viewing rapid sequences of human and non-human images. Data were collected across 2 sessions per subject with 3 runs per session, with visual feedback provided during the task.
